verses.text: As he sits with the elders, the Spirit lifts him to Jerusalem, to 'the image of jealousy' set in the temple gate. He is shown the truth. God reveals the hidden idolatry that provokes Him.
As he sits with the elders, the Spirit lifts him to Jerusalem, to 'the image of jealousy' set in the temple gate. He is shown the truth. God reveals the hidden idolatry that provokes Him.
As he sits with the elders, the Spirit lifts him to Jerusalem, to 'the image of jealousy' set in the temple gate. He is shown the truth. God reveals the hidden idolatry that provokes Him.
God's glory is there, yet He shows 'the great abominations' of Israel 'that I should go far off from my sanctuary.' Sin drives Him away. Persistent idolatry forces the holy God from His own house.
God's glory is there, yet He shows 'the great abominations' of Israel 'that I should go far off from my sanctuary.' Sin drives Him away. Persistent idolatry forces the holy God from His own house.
God's glory is there, yet He shows 'the great abominations' of Israel 'that I should go far off from my sanctuary.' Sin drives Him away. Persistent idolatry forces the holy God from His own house.
Digging through the wall, the prophet finds a hidden door; within are creeping things and idols portrayed all around. Secret corruption. Hidden sins are open before the eyes of God.

Digging through the wall, the prophet finds a hidden door; within are creeping things and idols portrayed all around. Secret corruption. Hidden sins are open before the eyes of God.
Digging through the wall, the prophet finds a hidden door; within are creeping things and idols portrayed all around. Secret corruption. Hidden sins are open before the eyes of God.
Seventy elders offer incense in the dark, saying, 'The LORD seeth us not.' Greater abominations remain. Practical atheism. Men sin freely when they imagine God does not see.
Seventy elders offer incense in the dark, saying, 'The LORD seeth us not.' Greater abominations remain. Practical atheism. Men sin freely when they imagine God does not see.
Seventy elders offer incense in the dark, saying, 'The LORD seeth us not.' Greater abominations remain. Practical atheism. Men sin freely when they imagine God does not see.
At the gate women weep for Tammuz, a heathen idol; yet greater abominations are still to come. Borrowed worship. God's house had been filled with the rites of the nations.
At the gate women weep for Tammuz, a heathen idol; yet greater abominations are still to come. Borrowed worship. God's house had been filled with the rites of the nations.

At the very temple door twenty-five men turn their backs on it to worship the sun in the east; therefore God will 'deal in fury,' and His eye 'shall not spare.' The climax of guilt. To worship the creature in God's own house is to provoke His full wrath.
